items.title: AI system intended use, deployment context, and named owner are documented for the current review window.
items.rationale: Documentary governance evidence with a named human attestor rather than a decidable runtime invariant.

items.title: AI risk management process and risk tolerances are documented for the current review window.
items.rationale: Documentary governance evidence for the current AI risk process remains attestation-routed.

items.title: Human oversight roles, escalation path, and override responsibility are documented for the current review window.
items.rationale: Human oversight remains documentary governance evidence in the current corridor.

items.title: Post-deployment monitoring ownership and incident escalation are documented for the current review window.
items.rationale: Post-deployment monitoring governance remains documentary in the current public corridor.

items.title: AI-generated content disclosure is enabled and enforcement checks are passing on the public assistant surface.
items.rationale: Deterministic disclosure configuration over a narrow transparency predicate.

items.title: AI-generated content provenance metadata is enabled and verification checks are passing on the public assistant surface.
items.rationale: Deterministic provenance-configuration state over a narrow transparency predicate.

items.title: AI evaluation scope, testing layers, and findings capture are documented for the current review window.
items.rationale: Evaluation design and findings remain documentary governance evidence in the current public corridor.

items.title: AI data sources, quality criteria, and lineage stewardship are documented for the current review window.
items.rationale: Data-quality and lineage governance stay documentary in the current public corridor.

items.title: The lawful-basis and purpose register is current for the scoped AI-assisted processing path.
items.rationale: Lawful-basis governance remains documentary but should still be explicit for AI-assisted processing.

items.title: A current privacy or AI impact assessment exists for the scoped AI-assisted processing path.
items.rationale: Impact-assessment evidence remains documentary governance evidence rather than proof.

items.title: High-impact human review triggers and a contest path are documented for the scoped AI-assisted decision path.
items.rationale: Human-review trigger evidence remains documentary governance evidence in the current AI corridor.

items.title: The legality and contestability of significant-effect automated decisions have been fully demonstrated for every in-scope use case.
items.rationale: The legality and sufficiency of high-impact automated-decision controls remain explicitly outside the machine-proof corridor.

note: Synthetic persisted routing artifact for the OpenCompliance prove/attest/judgment split.
